chapter_title.en: On Those Who Omit Wudu or Part of it, or Doubts in Its Completion

text: 133 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَبْقَى مِنْ وَجْهِهِ إِذَا تَوَضَّأَ مَوْضِعٌ لَمْ يُصِبْهُ اَلْمَاءُ فَقَالَ «يُجْزِيهِ أَنْ يَبُلَّهُ مِنْ بَعْضِ جَسَدِهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.133 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a man who, while performing wudu, misses a small area on his face that the water does not reach.
Imam (as) said: "It suffices for him to moisten it with water from another part of his body."
